title: Watching commuters at a Tokyo crosswalk
summary: Describe a few minutes spent watching people cross a busy intersection in Tokyo.
topic: Watching commuters at a Tokyo crosswalk
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) describing a busy crosswalk or street corner in a big city. Use present simple for habitual actions and present continuous for what is happening right now. Include prepositions of place.
hintStarters: Every weekday morning at about eight, the crossing in front of…
Right now, an old man is standing near the kerb while…
What surprises me most is how quiet…
sampleText: Every weekday morning at about eight, I sit at a small coffee shop on the second floor near Shibuya station. From the window I can see one of the busiest crosswalks in Tokyo. The light is red, and hundreds of people are waiting on both sides. Most of them are looking at their phones or holding takeaway coffee. When the light turns green, the whole crowd steps off the pavement at the same moment. From above it looks like two rivers flowing toward each other. The strange thing is how quiet it is. Nobody is shouting, and almost nobody is running, even though many people are in a hurry. Right now, an old man is standing near the kerb while younger commuters move calmly around him. A schoolgirl with big headphones is walking very slowly because she is reading something on her phone. After thirty seconds the crowd is gone, and the road is empty again. I drink my coffee and wait for the next light. I have lived in many cities, but I have never seen so many people share so little space so politely. The traffic light teaches everyone the same lesson at the same time.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: one of the busiest crosswalks in Tokyo
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một trong những vạch sang đường đông đúc nhất ở Tokyo
đang đợi ở cả hai bên
đang nhìn vào điện thoại của họ

sampleAnnotations.phrase: two rivers flowing toward each other
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: hai dòng sông chảy về phía nhau

sampleAnnotations.phrase: share so little space so politely
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: chia sẻ không gian nhỏ một cách lịch sự đến vậy
dạy cho mọi người cùng một bài học

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple vs present continuous
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng present simple cho thói quen ('I sit at a small coffee shop', 'the road is empty again') và present continuous cho hành động đang diễn ra ngay lúc này ('hundreds of people are waiting', 'a schoolgirl is walking very slowly'). Khi mô tả một cảnh, hai thì này thường đan xen nhau.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use present simple for habits ('I sit at a small coffee shop', 'the road is empty again') and present continuous for actions happening right now ('hundreds of people are waiting', 'a schoolgirl is walking very slowly'). When you describe a scene, the two tenses often weave together.

sampleTranslationVi: Mỗi sáng các ngày trong tuần vào khoảng tám giờ, tôi ngồi ở một quán cà phê nhỏ trên tầng hai gần ga Shibuya. Từ cửa sổ tôi có thể thấy một trong những vạch sang đường đông đúc nhất ở Tokyo. Đèn đỏ, và hàng trăm người đang đợi ở cả hai bên. Hầu hết họ đang nhìn vào điện thoại hoặc cầm cà phê mang đi. Khi đèn chuyển xanh, cả đám đông bước xuống khỏi vỉa hè cùng một lúc. Từ trên cao nhìn xuống trông như hai dòng sông chảy về phía nhau. Điều lạ là yên tĩnh đến mức nào. Không ai la hét, và hầu như không ai chạy, mặc dù nhiều người đang vội. Ngay lúc này, một ông cụ đang đứng gần lề đường trong khi những người đi làm trẻ hơn di chuyển bình tĩnh xung quanh ông. Một nữ sinh đeo tai nghe lớn đang đi rất chậm vì cô ấy đang đọc gì đó trên điện thoại. Sau ba mươi giây đám đông biến mất, và con đường lại trống không. Tôi uống cà phê và đợi đèn tiếp theo. Tôi đã sống ở nhiều thành phố, nhưng tôi chưa bao giờ thấy nhiều người chia sẻ không gian nhỏ một cách lịch sự đến vậy. Đèn giao thông dạy cho mọi người cùng một bài học vào cùng một lúc.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present continuous: 'Hundreds of people ___ on both sides.'
options: wait
are waiting
were waiting
have waited
mcQuestions.explanation: Action happening right now → present continuous.
mcQuestions.hint: Right now, in front of your eyes.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the present simple sentence that fits a habit.
options: Every weekday morning, I am sitting at a coffee shop.
Every weekday morning, I sit at a coffee shop.
Every weekday morning, I sat at a coffee shop.
Every weekday morning, I have sat at a coffee shop.
a person who travels regularly between home and work
a person who works inside a coffee shop
a person who only travels for holidays
a person who drives a taxi
mcQuestions.explanation: A 'commuter' is someone who travels to and from work regularly.

options: the time of day when traffic is busiest
the cheapest hour at a café
the time after midnight
the hour when shops close
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Rush hour' is the busiest traffic period of the day.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct preposition: 'people are waiting ___ both sides.'
options: in
at
on
by
mcQuestions.explanation: 'On both sides' is the natural collocation.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase means 'going quickly because you don't have much time'?
options: in a hurry
on a break
at a stop
by a light
mcQuestions.explanation: 'In a hurry' means rushing because you have little time.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural present continuous sentence.
options: An old man stands near the kerb.
An old man is stand near the kerb.
An old man is standing near the kerb.
An old man was standing near the kerb.
the part of the road where cars drive
the path along the side of a street for people to walk on
a small bridge over a river
a square in front of a building
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Pavement' = walking path next to a road.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence with correct word order.
options: Almost nobody is running, even though many people are in a hurry.
Almost nobody running is, even though many people are in a hurry.
Almost nobody is run, even though many people are in a hurry.
Nobody almost is running, even though many people are in a hurry.
mcQuestions.explanation: Subject + is + verb-ing is the correct order.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence shows the right use of 'how + adjective' to express surprise?
options: The strange thing is how quiet is it.
The strange thing is how is it quiet.
The strange thing is how quiet it is.
The strange thing is how it quiet is.
mcQuestions.hint: Subject and verb stay in normal order.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does 'step off the pavement' mean?
options: to walk down from the sidewalk onto the road
to climb a staircase
to enter a shop
to stop at a red light
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Step off' = move down or away from a surface, here from the sidewalk to the road.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do you think some cities feel calm even when they are crowded, while others feel chaotic at the same density of people?
